rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Objection To Bond And Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Sustaining Plaintiff'S Objection To The Bond [Code Of Civ. Proc. 995.950]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 20, 2024 line 12. PLAINTIFF STEPHEN WEISSBERG MOTION / OBJECTION TO BOND AND NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR AN ORDER SUSTAINING PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO THE BOND [CODE OF CIV. PROC. 995.950] Hearing Required. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KAYLA WARE VS. GAETANI REAL ESTATE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion To Add Doe Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 20, 2024 line 2. PLAINTIFF KAYLA WARE MOTION / NOTICE OF MOTION TO ADD DOE DEFENDANTS is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 for a motion set for hearing on March 20, 2024 and filed and e-served on February 28, 2024. The required notice was 16 court days, plus 2 court days for service. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: OKSANA CLEM VS. 1140 HARRISON ASSOCIATES LP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Remove Collections And Invalidate Lease And Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 20, 2024 line 3. PLAINTIFF OKSANA CLEM MOTION TO REMOVE COLLECTIONS AND INVALIDATE LEASE AND SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party failed to state any legal grounds for the motion in the Notice of Motion as required by the California Rules of Court. Points and Authorities are devoid of any authorities addressing the relief requested. It is not clear what motion the Plaintiff is attempting to bring, but none of the motions that Court could construe this as would be procedurally or substantively proper, e.g. Motion for Summary Judgment, Preliminary Injunction, Judgment on the Pleadings, etc. The Court further notes that the moving party failed to comply with the formatting requirements of the California Rules of Court, e.g. the briefs are single-spaced. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA VS. MATHEW MAHONEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 20, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT MATHEW MAHONEY FINANCIAL DISTRESS DECLARATION is DENIED. Per the verified Complaint the operative 10 Day Notice was served on September 15, 2023 demanding rent for the months of October 2022 to September 2023. Remedies under CCP 1179.03(h) are not applicable to this time period. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
